title_original: Quobly And Entropica Labs Partner To Accelerate Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing
summary_original: Quobly, a French silicon-based quantum computing company, and Singapore’s Entropica Labs have signed a strategic Memorandum of Understanding in Singapore to co-develop fault-tolerant quantum computing technologies. The partnership will integrate Quobly’s silicon quantum processor architecture with Entropica’s fault-tolerant software from the earliest design phases, focusing on advanced compiler and error correction technologies for real-world scalability. Quobly’s CEO Maud Vinet stated the collaboration “strengthens our ability to build scalable quantum systems by combining silicon hardware with fault-tolerant software.” Entropica’s CEO Tommaso Demarie noted the shift toward system-level fault tolerance as processors scale. Quobly’s recent €21 million funding round supports its Q100T program and fuels Asian expansion, positioning Singapore as a key regional hub.
